original: Seven-year-old boy undergoes surgery after 526 teeth are found inside his mouth in Chennai, India
First footage of white giraffes is posted by the Hirola Conservation Program in northeastern Kenya
Lewis Carrollpublishes the children's novel "Alice's Adventures in Wonderland"
SpaceXDragon capsule carrying NASA astronauts Doug Hurley and Bob Behnken splashes down in the Gulf of Mexico, completing the first commercial crewed mission

Fossils from the Ica Desert, Peru, may belong to the heaviest creature that ever lived, surpassing the blue whale. Named Perucetus colossus, its heavier bones suggest it could have weighed 85 to 340 tonnes
TikTok, the video-sharing social network, becomes available worldwide after merging with Musical.ly
Carl David Andersondiscovers and photographs a positron, the first known antiparticle
First successful gene editing in human embryos to repair disease-causing mutation reported by scientists in "Nature"
Oldest library in Germany confirmed unearthed in Cologne dating to 2 AD, possibly holding 20,000 scrolls

After 3½ days suffering exhaustion, lack of water, and shark attacks in the Philippine Sea, the surviving crew of USS Indianapolis are spotted by Wilbur “Chuck” Gwinn, a PV-1 Ventura pilot on a routine sector search; 316 survive

Vitaly Scherbo of Belarus becomes the first gymnast to win six gold medals at one Olympics in Barcelona and the first to win four golds in one day's competition (pommel horse, vault, rings, and parallel bars)
American athleteJackie Joyner-Kerseebeats her own world heptathlon record by 13 points (7,161) in Houston
NASA confirms Voyager 2's discovery of three more moons of Neptune, designated temporarily as 1989 N2, 1989 N3, and 1989 N24
Army Air Corps formed as Army takes first delivery from Wright Brothers
US issues the first Lincoln penny, featuring a portrait ofAbraham Lincolnon the front and wheat stalks on the reverse
The Beatles perform their first gig as the house band at Liverpool's Cavern Club
Formal signing of the US Declaration of Independence by 56 people (date most accepted by modern historians)
John Tyndall, Irish physicist who demonstrated why the sky is blue and proved that the Earth's atmosphere has a greenhouse effect, born in Leighlinbridge, County Carlow, Ireland (d. 1893)
English explorerHenry Hudsonenters the bay later named after him, the Hudson Bay
George A. Wheeler is granted a US patent for a prototype of the escalator
First trial run of San Francisco cable car in Clay Street between Kearny and Jones
First parachute jump in US by Frenchman Louis-Charles Guille over Jersey City
Oakland A's outfielderRickey Hendersonsteals his 100th MLB base of the season in a 6-5 win against Seattle, becoming the first to steal 100 bases twice in the modern era
Australian Kaylee McKeown joins Roland Matthes of Germany (1968, 72) as the only swimmers to achieve the Olympic 100/200 m backstroke double when McKeown wins the 200m event in Paris
Jeff Blatnick becomes the first American to win a gold medal in Greco-Roman wrestling at the Los Angeles Olympics while in remission for Hodgkin’s disease
The star-studded United States men's basketball team,Dream Team III, beat Yugoslavia 95-69 to win the gold medal at the Atlanta Olympics
Cuban super-heavyweight Teofilo Stevenson becomes the first fighter to win three Olympic gold medals in the same division, scoring a 4-1 points decision over Pyotr Zayev (Soviet Union) in Moscow
American swimmer Rebecca Soni becomes the first woman to win the 200 m breaststroke twice with a world record of 2:19.59 at the London Olympics
Michael Phelpsends the World Swimming Championships in Rome with his fifth gold medal as part of the US 4 x 100 m medley relay team, which records the 43rd world record of the controversial meet
Fanny Blankers-Koen of the Netherlands wins the 100 m in 11.9 at the London Olympics, her first of unprecedented three individual track and field gold medals
Philippa Schuyler, African-American child classical piano prodigy, composer, and journalist, born in Harlem, New York (d. 1967)
MESSENGER spacecraft performs an Earth flyby
Space Shuttle STS-43 (Atlantis 9) launches
US Lunar Orbiter 5 is launched and enters lunar orbit on August 5
Lovell Telescope's first light (first astronomical image) by what was then the world's largest steerable telescope at Jodrell Bank, England
Beijing records its heaviest rainfall in 140 years, registering 744.8 millimeters (29.3 inches) between July 29th and August 2nd as Typhoon Doksuri passes through
104°F (40°C) in Providence, Rhode Island (state record)
107°F (42°C) in Chester/New Bedford, Massachusetts (state record)
Saudi Arabia announces new rules for women, including allowing them to travel independently abroad without a male guardian's permission
Mary Prince Day is established in Bermuda, recognizing Mary Prince, the first known Black woman to relate the story of her life, which helps hasten the abolition of slavery in the British Empire
United Arab Emirates starts up the Arab world's first nuclear power station at the Barakah plant
More than a billion people around the world need glasses and 36 million are blind, according to a new study published in "The Lancet"
Fields Medal for mathematics is awarded to Caucher Birkar (his is stolen minutes later), Alessio Figalli, Peter Scholze, and Akshay Venkatesh

Ron Hansen (Washington Senators) and Tim Cullen (Chicago White Sox) become the first MLB players to be traded for each other twice in the same season; they had been traded in February in opposite directions
Jewish survivors of Kovono Ghetto emerge from their bunker
Elisha Grey, American electrical engineer and inventor (Telephone), born in Barnesville, Ohio (d. 1901)
Bill Scott, American voice actor, producer and writer (Mr Peabody, Bullwinkle), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1985)

The film "In the Heat of the Night," based on John Ball's novel of the same name, directed by Norman Jewison, and starringSidney PoitierandRod Steiger, premieres in New York and wins five Academy Awards in 1968, including Best Picture and Best Actor for Steiger
MLB conducts the first test of bright yellow baseballs during Brooklyn Dodgers vs. St. Louis Cardinals doubleheader
Battle of the Nile: British Royal Navy under AdmiralHoratio Nelsonfurther decimates the French fleet
Lt.John F. Kennedy's PT boat 109 sinks in the Solomon Islands

The largest trade in NBA history is completed as five teams combine to swap 13 players; the deal brings Antoine Walker and Jason Williams to Miami and leads to the Heat's first-ever championship that season
South African cricket batsman Graeme Smith blazes 259, and fast bowler Makhaya Ntini takes five wickets in each England innings as Proteas win the second Test by an innings and 92 runs at Lord's
Super Rugby Final, ANZ Stadium, Sydney: NSW Waratahs win their first SR title in a nail-biting 33-32 home effort against New Zealand's Canterbury Crusaders
Lee Elder fires a record 11-under-par 61 on his way to winning the Senior PGA Merrill Lynch/Golf Digest Commemorative at Sleepy Hollow CC; the record stands for 11 years
Danish swimmer Greta Andersen (1:06.3) touches out American Ann Curtis by 0.2 seconds in the women's 100 m freestyle at the London Olympics
NYC's recently established Landmarks Preservation Commission designates Grand Central Terminal train station as a landmark, thereby protecting its facade and interior architectural features
Jordan and Iraq dissolve their Arab Federation after 3 months
First airplane train: airplane tows three mail gliders behind it
Swiss alpine explorer Horace Bénédict de Saussure becomes the third person to reach the top of Mont Blanc
